target: data/wins.json post_hoc fields + data/sections.json part2b SEC-2b.3
summary: Audit of post_hoc tags: 10 of original 12 post_hoc=false tags have already been corrected (prior to this run). The 2 remaining (WIN-031, WIN-044) are correctly tagged. Zero WINs have post_hoc_evidence fields. This expansion provides evidence rationale for all 70 WINs and confirms the section prose (SEC-2b.3) already addresses the curmudgeon's major concerns about dome prospective/backtested framework, derivation question, and Newton retrodiction defense.

post_hoc_false_wins.rationale: Theological assertion ("cosmic mountain" at north pole from biblical texts). There is no measurable observation being retrodicted — the claim is unfalsifiable by design. post_hoc is not applicable to claims without empirical content; false is the appropriate tag since the concept of "stated after the data" presupposes there IS data.
post_hoc_false_wins.post_hoc_evidence: Unfalsifiable theological claim with no empirical observation to be post-hoc about. The "cosmic mountain" is a biblical/mythological assertion, not a prediction of measurable phenomena. No measurement can confirm or disconfirm this claim, so the prediction/retrodiction distinction does not apply.

post_hoc_false_wins.claim: Firmament Scaling Function from V12 geometry
post_hoc_false_wins.rationale: The FSF is derived from dome V12 geometry (H(r)/r scaling). The predicted eclipse path deviations have NOT yet been tested — the discriminating test is the August 2026 eclipse. This is one of only 2 WINs where post_hoc=false AND derives_from_dome=true, making it the dome's strongest methodological case. However, the Self-Contradicted verdict holds because the FSF predicts firmament heights that conflict with other dome parameters (sun altitude 5,733 km exceeds FSF-predicted firmament height at that radius).
post_hoc_false_wins.post_hoc_evidence: Derived from dome V12 geometry (Firmament Scaling Function H(r)/r). The eclipse path predictions are prospective — registered before the August 2026 eclipse. This is a genuine dome-derived prediction with a future test window. Tagged post_hoc=false because the predicted values were computed from dome equations before confirming data exists.

post_hoc_true_bulk_evidence.description: The 68 WINs tagged post_hoc=true fall into clear categories. Rather than adding 68 individual rationale strings, the decider should apply category-based evidence templates.

categories.name: Pre-existing observational data
categories.template: Observation [X] published by [source] in [year], predating dome model incorporation. The dome states the already-known value as a "prediction" without derivation from its own equations.

categories.name: Dome weekly prospective (trivial extrapolations)
categories.template: Registered 2026-03-06 and confirmed by 2026-03-12. Sets threshold based on recent NOAA/INTERMAGNET trend and checks one week later. Technically timestamped before confirmation, but the prediction is a trivial extrapolation of an ongoing trend (e.g., SAA continues declining, Schumann stays at 7.83 Hz). No dome equation produces the threshold value.
examples: WIN-035 (SAA African < 21,795 nT — SAA declining ~30 nT/yr)

categories.name: Cosmological/V51.0 additions
categories.template: Observation [X] published in astronomical literature [years/decades] before V51.0 incorporated it. The dome relabels standard astrophysical phenomena using dome vocabulary (e.g., "aetheric" Hubble Law, "firmament" lensing).
examples: WIN-048 (CMB Axis of Evil — de Oliveira-Costa et al. 2004)

categories.name: Definitions/tautologies tagged derives_from_dome=true but still post_hoc
categories.template: Defines a coordinate system or topological zone within dome geometry. Not an empirical prediction — it is a self-referential definition that could have been stated at any time. Post_hoc because the definitions were crafted after the author knew the observational facts they needed to accommodate.

recommended_data_patches.value: Unfalsifiable theological claim (biblical "cosmic mountain" at north pole). No empirical observation exists to be post-hoc about. The prediction/retrodiction distinction does not apply to unfalsifiable assertions.
Derived from dome V12 geometry (Firmament Scaling Function H(r)/r). Eclipse path predictions registered before August 2026 test window. Genuine dome-derived prediction with future empirical test.

section_prose_assessment.details: SEC-2b.3 prose has been substantially rewritten since the curmudgeon's cycle 1 review. The following curmudgeon concerns are now addressed in the current prose:
addressed_concerns: Critical #2: Dome's own prospective/backtested distinction is now acknowledged ("dome's wins page explicitly distinguishes PROSPECTIVE from backtested")
Major #3: Derivation question now leads the section ("The derivation question (the central issue)")
Major #4: The 4 derives_from_dome WINs are named and individually assessed (WIN-020, 044, 057, 058)
Moderate #6: Pipeline metaphor now has qualifier ("Not every WIN follows this exact sequence")
Moderate #7: Timestamp argument now distinguishes two cases (58 WINs vs 9 prospective)
Moderate #8: Newton/Kepler retrodiction defense is acknowledged and rebutted
section_prose_assessment.remaining_concern: Critical #1 (post_hoc=false audit) — the tags have been corrected from 12 to 2, but zero WINs have post_hoc_evidence fields. This expansion provides the evidence rationale for decider to integrate.

kernel_of_truth: The dome model's author demonstrates genuine methodological awareness: distinguishing prospective from backtested, building timestamp infrastructure, registering predictions before test windows close. This is more epistemological rigor than most alternative cosmology models attempt. The 2 correctly-tagged post_hoc=false WINs (031, 044) reflect real category distinctions — a theological claim genuinely has no observation to be post-hoc about, and a geometric derivation with a future test really is prospective. Our credibility depends on acknowledging these distinctions honestly rather than bulldozing everything into post_hoc=true.
holes_addressed: Critical #1: Audited all post_hoc=false WINs — 10/12 already corrected, remaining 2 (WIN-031, WIN-044) validated as correct
Critical #1 (secondary): Provided post_hoc_evidence rationale for WIN-031 and WIN-044, plus category templates for bulk addition to the 68 post_hoc=true WINs
Critical #2 through Moderate #8: Confirmed already addressed in current section prose
new_evidence_added: Systematic post_hoc_evidence rationale framework with 4 category templates covering all 70 WINs
Explicit validation that WIN-031 and WIN-044 are correctly tagged post_hoc=false with documented reasoning

anticipated_objections.objection: The 2 post_hoc=false WINs (out of 70) mean your section claims 97% are post-hoc, not the published ~93%. Shouldn't you just tag everything true and avoid the complexity?
anticipated_objections.response: Intellectual honesty requires recognizing genuine category distinctions. WIN-031 is unfalsifiable — the concept of "post-hoc" presupposes there IS data. WIN-044 has a genuine prospective test window. Bulldozing these into post_hoc=true would be exactly the kind of sloppy categorization we criticize the dome model for.

anticipated_objections.objection: Adding post_hoc_evidence to 70 WINs is busywork — the section prose already makes the argument.
anticipated_objections.response: The curmudgeon's point is valid: if a dome defender examines our tags, finding zero evidence fields undermines the infrastructure's credibility. The 2 post_hoc=false WINs need individual evidence immediately. The 68 post_hoc=true WINs can use category templates applied in batches — this is decider/infrastructure work, not expansion work.

current_word_count: N/A — data patch, not prose rewrite
action_items_for_decider: Apply post_hoc_evidence fields to WIN-031 and WIN-044 per recommended_data_patches
Schedule bulk post_hoc_evidence addition for 68 post_hoc=true WINs using category templates (lower priority, can be done incrementally)
No section prose changes needed — SEC-2b.3 already addresses curmudgeon concerns
